what_it_does: Documents a dated architecture transmission and its expanded interpretation for representing a repository as an addressed, typed, queryable graph with registries, typed edges, attachments, ghost nodes, timestamps, and deterministic agent operations.
observations: The file is split into an expanded hydration section and a preserved verbatim transmission from Tim dated 2026-06-17.
It frames repository paths as addresses relative to the project root and file or folder kinds as registry-resolved types.
It defines address registries, type registries, special-name registries, language registries, and project registries as related parts of the substrate.
It treats folders and files as Directory descendants connected by contains and contained-by edges.
It describes read-and-report agents that operate over allocated territory, emit objective per-file structured outputs, and avoid judgement tasks.
It specifies attachments as the general mechanism for observations, decisions, notes, edge records, histories, and other typed facts attached to addresses.
It introduces ghost nodes for expected or implicated addresses that do not exist.
It includes an operational status note claiming generated address/type registries, repo-census outputs, ghost nodes, and wayfinder artifacts.
standouts: The repository is modeled as a typed graph whose nodes are filesystem addresses and whose behavior is resolved from registries.
The dispatch model is territory-based coverage rather than search-based retrieval.
Zero counts, timestamps, stable signatures, and ghost nodes are treated as explicit facts in the substrate.
The document connects agent orchestration, parsing, type rules, structured outputs, and wayfinding into one registry-resolved system.
conventions: Uses numbered sections to expand a compressed source message into discrete architectural claims.
Uses project-specific principle names such as derive-never-place, registry-is-truth, equal-and-opposite typed edges, and two-halves.
Keeps the authored transmission verbatim after the interpreted hydration.
Uses bold emphasis for key substrate terms and italic framing notes.
Distinguishes authored source text from interpretive expansion through headings and prose framing.
concerns: The file mixes stable architecture description with dated implementation/status claims in prose.
Some concrete schemas, registry field shapes, and checker criteria are described conceptually but not specified as machine-readable contracts.
The verbatim section contains transcription errors and rough wording, intentionally preserved, which may complicate direct extraction.
notes: Briefing view: this file is the conceptual contract for a registry-resolved repository substrate and the preserved source transmission behind it.
questions: What exact schema should address, type, attachment, edge, ghost-node, and registry-rule records use?
Which parts of the dated status note are historical record versus current expected substrate state?
How are stable in-file signatures generated and resolved back to current line positions?
What criteria does the checker use beyond technical schema validation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it preserves the compact transmission and expands it into a structured architecture document for the substrate.
intention_signals: It seems intended to turn a dense architecture message into durable project language that agents can use to build, query, extend, and navigate a repository substrate.
novelty: The distinct element is the combination of filesystem-relative addressing, type registries, typed attachments, ghost nodes, timestamped observations, and agent dispatch into one queryable repository substrate.
contribution: It contributes the conceptual model for an addressed and typed repository graph, including registry-governed operations, objective census outputs, attachment records, ghost-node handling, temporal facts, and wayfinding views that a fused system would keep as substrate semantics.
summary_for_embedding: This is a substrate architecture document that expands and preserves a 2026-06-17 transmission describing repositories as addressed, typed, queryable graphs governed by registries, typed edges, attachments, ghost nodes, timestamps, and deterministic agent workflows.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to make a compact architecture transmission usable as shared project guidance for building and operating a registry-resolved repository wayfinding substrate.
